name: Bestest Buddy

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bestest Buddy 1.1.2

Patch release fixing two bugs found in a full-project code review and field testing. No action needed — no data or settings changes.

### Fixed: buddy commented on files you never opened at app launch (#39)

Obsidian replays a `create` event for every existing file in the vault while its index initializes at startup. The plugin subscribed to note creations during load, so every launch fired a stampede of "new note" events: the buddy would greet you with commentary about an arbitrary vault file — often the last one in the list — that you never opened. The replay also flushed your previous session's writing history out of the buddy's memory and caused one settings-file write per vault file at startup.

The plugin now starts listening for note creations only after the vault's startup index is built. Genuinely new notes react exactly as before, launches are quiet, and session memory carries over between sessions.

### Fixed: overlapping requests could double-spend API calls and race the speech bubble (#29)

Three paths could start a reply while another was already in flight — chatty-mode ticks, command-palette asks (the palette, unlike the panel buttons, has no disabled state), and hatching. When two requests overlapped, the first to finish re-enabled the UI while the second was still pending, and whichever reply resolved last silently overwrote the other. Every entry point is now gated by an atomic busy check — including mid-request windows during note reads and event logging. A command-palette ask while the buddy is thinking now shows a "buddy is busy" notice instead of silently overlapping.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/nateking-dev/bestest-buddy/compare/1.1.1...1.1.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bestest Buddy 1.1.1

Patch release fixing one bug found in a full-project code review. No action needed — no data or settings changes.

### Fixed: false revision reactions when following a link in the same pane (#28)

Obsidian does not fire `active-leaf-change` when the same leaf navigates to a different file, so the plugin kept the previous note's size and session counters. The first keystroke in a shorter note then read as a huge deletion and fired a spurious `revision_spike` — an unwanted LLM call, wrong mood/session state, and a polluted event history. Editing a long note, clicking a link to a short note in the same pane, and typing could make the buddy react as if you had just deleted half your draft.

File switches are now detected in the edit path itself, keyed on the file path, and the per-file typing state resets there. Genuine large deletions within the same note still react as before.

Also adds the first regression tests for the event-detection layer, including a vitest stub for the `obsidian` package that unblocks broader behavioral test coverage (#35).

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/nateking-dev/bestest-buddy/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bestest Buddy 1.1.0

This release fixes two issues found in a code review of 1.0.0 — one privacy, one API cost. No action needed: existing data is cleaned up automatically on first load.

### Privacy: note content is no longer stored in plugin data (#26)

Previously, the event log persisted to `data.json` could include excerpts of your notes and your direct chat messages — up to 8 KB per event, unencrypted, inside your vault's `.obsidian` folder.

- Note excerpts and chat messages are now stripped from every event before it is saved; the persisted log contains only event type, timestamp, and note title.
- A one-time data migration (schema v2) scrubs previously stored note content from `data.json` the first time the plugin loads after updating.
- Cursor excerpts sent to the LLM are now capped at 320 characters everywhere.
- The **Include current note context** setting description now accurately states that it applies to both direct replies and ambient reactions, and that note content is never stored in plugin data.

### Fixed: hover-petting could fire unbounded LLM calls (#26)

Petting the buddy by hovering over the sprite triggered an LLM reaction with no cooldown or in-flight guard, so repeated hovering could stack up paid API requests. Pet reactions are now rate-limited to one every 30 seconds and skipped while another request is in flight — the hearts animation still plays on every pet.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/nateking-dev/bestest-buddy/compare/1.0.0...1.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Bestest Buddy 1.0.0

A vault-scoped writing companion for Obsidian. Bestest Buddy hatches a persistent ASCII pet for each vault, watches how you write, and reacts with short, in-context observations — a companion that lives in your sidebar, not a chatbot you converse with.

It's built around three principles:

- **One buddy per vault** — generated from a vault seed and stable until you reset it.
- **Reactive, not conversational** — it responds to writing bursts, pauses, revisions, new notes, and direct questions with brief quips.
- **Lightweight and non-intrusive** — short replies, a compact panel, cooldowns to avoid nagging, and **no autonomous edits to your notes**.

```
   .----.
  / ×  × \      Capy Scribbles
 |   --   |     Common Capybara
  `-____-´
```

## Features

**Vault-scoped companion.** Each vault gets its own deterministically generated buddy with persistent traits:
- **Name** and **personality** (LLM-generated, with a local fallback)
- **Species** — one of 18: duck, goose, blob, cat, dragon, octopus, owl, penguin, turtle, snail, ghost, axolotl, capybara, cactus, robot, rabbit, mushroom, chonk
- **Rarity** — common, uncommon, rare, epic, legendary, mythic (weighted rolls, so a mythic is genuinely rare and gets an animated rainbow sprite, background, and nameplate)
- **Hat** — none, crown, top hat, propeller, halo, wizard, beanie, tiny duck
- **Eye style** — one of `·` `✦` `×` `◉` `@` `°`
- **Shiny** state and an optional custom **sprite color**
- **Stats** — GRAMMARING, PATIENCE, CHAOS, WISDOM, SNARK

**Sidebar panel.** A dedicated right-sidebar view with a header (name, rarity, species, hat, shiny, personality), an animated ASCII sprite, a speech bubble that reveals replies word-by-word then fades, session/mood indicators, pattern hints, action buttons (hatch/pet, mute/unmute, ask about the current note, reset), and a compact direct-chat input. **Minimal mode** shows only the sprite.

**Animated sprite.** Idle loop with occasional blinks, a speaking animation while a bubble is visible, a petting animation with floating hearts, and a quieter "settled" microstate after a long idle period.

**Ambient writing reactions.** When enabled and not muted, the buddy occasionally reacts to vault activity — opening a daily note, creating a note, writing in a burst, sustaining a session, making a large revision cut, pausing, or returning after a pause. Reactions are gated by cooldown, probability, session state, and your snark level, so they punctuate rather than interrupt. In **Chatty** mode a periodic tick lets it comment on the text near your cursor.

**Direct chat & note awareness.** Message the buddy from the panel or ask it about the current note. Replies are short, grounded in the buddy's personality and session state, and — when note context is enabled — informed by your current selection (or the full note if nothing is selected).

**Session, mood & pattern tracking.** A lightweight model of your session drives the UI and reactions:
- **Session modes:** idle, starting, flowing, revising, stuck, returning
- **Moods:** quiet, curious, pleased, concerned, sleepy
- **Patterns:** repeated long pauses, revision loop, sustained momentum, stop-start session, fresh-page exploration

**Customization.** Override any visible trait via the Customize commands — species, eye style, hat, rarity, sprite color (presets or a custom `#rrggbb` hex), shiny, and individual stats. Overrides are stored separately from the generated companion, so "reset appearance to generated" restores the original at any time.

## LLM providers & fallbacks

The buddy uses an LLM for two jobs: generating its name and personality at hatch time, and generating short reactions and direct replies.

| Provider | Endpoint | Example model |
| --- | --- | --- |
| OpenAI | `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses` | `gpt-4.1-mini` |
| Claude (Anthropic) | `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` | `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` |

If no API key is configured or a request fails, hatching falls back to local name/personality generation and reactions fall back to local rule-based text — the plugin remains fully functional without any API key, just less varied.

## Privacy & data

- All state lives in the plugin's per-vault `data.json` (vault seed, hatched companion, appearance overrides, mute state, the 20 most recent events, mood/session state, settings).
- The only network calls are to the provider endpoint you configure. Note title and excerpts are sent only when *Include current note context* is enabled, and direct-chat context is truncated before sending.
- API keys are stored in plugin data and masked in the settings UI.

## What it does not do (by design)

- No autonomous edits or writes to your notes.
- No long-form, assistant-style responses.
- No full chat-transcript UI.
- No syncing buddies across vaults.
- No network calls other than to the configured provider.

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the assets below and copy them into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/bestest-buddy/`, then enable **Bestest Buddy** under Settings → Community plugins. Requires Obsidian **1.7.2+**. Works on desktop and mobile.

## Notes

This 1.0.0 release incorporates the Obsidian community-plugin review fixes: `revealLeaf` awaited with `minAppVersion` 1.7.2, settings headings that don't repeat the plugin name, no unsupported CSS font keywords, native `module.builtinModules` in the build, void-returning modal callbacks, and removal of unnecessary assertions and unused symbols.

Author: **Nathan King**, with thanks to contributor **Owen McGrath**. Licensed under MIT.
